other_observers: Many. I saw it with Brandon Percival, Tony Leukering, and Doug Faulkner.; Brandon Percival first observed the bird.
description: The bird was a medium-sized buteoine-like hawk, but slightly slimmer and with a longer tail. It was dark brown above and below with chestnut shoulders and thighs. The chestnut on some of the scapulars were dark-brown centered. There was some white feathering acoss portions of the breast, belly, and sides of neck. The throat was also mostly white. The bill was a bluish-gray, darker toward the tip. Evident was the large, bright yellow facial patch and gape. There was a thin yellow eye-ring surround the dark brown eyes. The long tail was mostly black, except for hite bases to the rectrices and a broad white terminal tail band. When in flight, you could see the brown primaries and secondaries and that the bird was in active molt. The undersides of the wing were mostly dark, except for rusty coverts and a whitish patch to the inner primaries.Behaviors: Bird was nervous and stayed hidden, prched in a Siberian Elm tree. It later flushed from that tree and relocated within the confines of an Austrian Pine near Brandon Percival's house.Call: None heardPlumage: juvenal molting to adult
photographer: Photographer: Larry Semo, Larry Semo, Larry Semo, Larry Semo, Larry Semo
